items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center building is cleared to construct at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard. The facility will expand data infrastructure capacity in San José, a core industry for the region.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A expansion permitted on Blossom Hill Road
items.summary: Chick-fil-A received a permit for a $810,000 demolition and addition project at 1162 Blossom Hill Road, indicating a renovation and likely enlargement of the existing location.

items.headline: Congregation Kol Emeth permitted to add glass above trellis
items.summary: The religious institution at 4175 Manuela Avenue received approval to increase lot coverage by adding glass above a portion of an existing trellis. The project involves a minor expansion of covered space at the congregation's facility.

items.headline: City approves Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: The City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust to install "Seeds of Peace," an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo, at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument is now authorized for placement at the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget set for FY 2026-27
items.summary: The Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget and annual assessment. This sets funding and assessment levels for the business district's operations and services in the coming fiscal year.

items.headline: City Council updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: Cupertino City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the General Plan covering 2015–2040. The General Plan guides zoning, development, and city services, so changes to the health and safety section could affect future policies on emergency preparedness, environmental hazards, or public health infrastructure.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: Milpitas City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. The appointment followed a formal selection process and fills the city's top administrative position.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates urban water master plan and drought contingency
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed the Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents set Sunnyvale's water supply strategy and drought response protocols.
